title: Koenigia weyrichii
taxonomy: Koenigia weyrichii is classified within the family Polygonaceae. This family is a part of the order Polygonales. The class to which Koenigia weyrichii belongs is Magnoliopsida. The phylum is Magnoliophyta, and the kingdom is Plantae. It is a member of the genus Koenigia.

parts: Koenigia weyrichii, also known as Tibetan fireweed, is used in traditional medicine. intro: Koenigia weyrichii, also known as Tibetian Koenigia, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Polygonaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Koenigia weyrichii include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or made into a powder for the treatment of various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
